title: Neottia ehrenbergii
intro: Neottia ehrenbergii, also known as Ehrenberg's tooth-orkid, is a species of orchid native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia, characterized by its delicate white flowers and thin, elongated leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, boasting several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Neottia ehrenbergii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Neottia ehrenbergii is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Neottia, which includes a group of terrestrial orchids commonly known as the 'bee orchids'. The species is native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. Within the genus Neottia, it is further classified as Neottia ehrenbergii, a distinctive species characterized by its unique flower structure. It is a member of the tribe Neottieae, which comprises several species of terrestrial orchids with similar growth habits.
